fields.title: SCHADS Award Calculation Engine — Specification
fields.content:
SCHADS Award Calculation Engine — Specification

Authoritative source — SCHADS engine Ticket. The engine Ticket governs wherever the Penalty or Rules sheets differ: meal allowance is $15.54, travel is $0.99 per km, and sleepover disturbance is paid at the overtime rate. The figures in this document have been reconciled to the Ticket.

The 11-step SCHADS pay-calculation logic, input schema, multiplier matrix, partitioning rules and the $444.54 validation benchmark.

Ticket: SCHADS Award Calculation Engine (v16.0)
1. Objective
To build a payroll logic engine that partitions raw time-logs into SCHADS-compliant line items by evaluating 11 sequential logic steps.
2. System Input Schema
Field | Type | Description
employee_stream | Enum | SOCIAL_COMMUNITY_SERVICES, HOME_CARE, CRISIS_ACCOMMODATION, FAMILY_DAY_CARE
classification_level | Integer | 1 through 8.
pay_point | Integer | 1 through 4 (varies by level).
3. The 11-Step Logic Sequence
The engine must process every shift through these steps in exact order. Level 1 (Step 4) overrides all temporal loadings.
Step 1: Minimum Engagement (Rounding) — [PRIMARY]
Rule: Every standalone engagement must be at least 2.0 hours.  (a)          social and community services employees (except when undertaking disability services work)—3 hours;
Logic: If duration < 2.0h, add "Paid Gap Time" to reach 2.0h.  (b)         all other employees—2 hours.
Separation: Engagements separated by a Sleepover (Step 2) are treated as distinct. You cannot sum a pre-sleepover hour and a post-sleepover hour to meet this minimum.
Step 2: Sleepover Anchor & Partitioning
Step 2: Sleepover Anchor & PartitioningTrigger: is_sleepover = True (10 PM – 6 AM).Allowance: 
Flat $60.02.Evening Trigger: If a pre-sleepover block ends at 22:00, the entire block uses the Evening Rate ($1.375$ Casual / $1.125$ Perm).
Action: Apply flat $60.02 allowance.
The Evening Trigger: If a shift ends at the Sleepover start (22:00), the entire block preceding it must use the Evening Rate ($1.375$ Casual / $1.125$ Perm) because 22:00 is > 20:00.
Step 3: Sleepover Disturbance
Trigger: Work during the 10 PM – 6 AM window.
Rule: Minimum 1.0 hour per disturbance.
Rate: Use Overtime Rate.  (unless Weekend/OT applies).
Step 4: Public Holiday & Weekend (Highest Rate Wins)
Logic: If the timestamp is Sat, Sun, or PH, ignore all Evening/Night/Ordinary loadings.
Multipliers (Perm/Casual): * Sat: 1.50 / 1.75
Sun: 2.00 / 2.25
PH: 2.50 / 2.75
Step 5: Meal Allowance ( tenant based)
Trigger: Total shift > 5 hours without a break, or Overtime > 2 hours.
Value: $15.54 (Taxable).
Step 6: Uniform & Laundry ( tenant based)
Value: $1.23 (Uniform) and $0.32 (Laundry) per shift.
Step 7: 10-Hour Break Safety
Trigger: If the gap between the end of the last shift and the start of the current shift is < 10 hours.
Penalty: Pay all hours of the current shift at overtime Rate until a 10-hour break is provided.
Step 8: 12-Hour Span Penalty (The "P" Variable)
Trigger: In a "Broken Shift," if Last_Segment_End minus First_Segment_Start > 12 hours.
Penalty: Pay overtime rate for all hours worked beyond the 12th hour of that span.
Step 9: Daily Overtime
Trigger: Active work > 10 hours in a 24-hour window.
Rate: First 2 hours at 1.5x, thereafter 2.0x.
Step 10: Weekly/Fortnightly Overtime ( tenant based) ( week will start from monday)
Trigger: Cumulative hours > 38 hour (Week) or > 76 hour (Fortnight).
Step 11: Travel / KM Allowance
Value: $0.99 per KM.
4. Multiplier Matrix (Casual vs. Permanent)
Category | Time Trigger (Weekday) | Permanent | Casual (Inc 25%)
Ordinary | 06:00 – 20:00 | 1 | 1.25
Evening | finishes between 20:00-24:00 | 1.125 | 1.375
Night | 00:00 – 06:00 | 1.15 | 1.4
5. Partitioning Logic for Developers
Boundary | Logic Name | Action

fields.title: SCHADS Award Calculation Engine — Specification
fields.content:
SCHADS Award Calculation Engine — Specification (continued)

Authoritative source — SCHADS engine Ticket. The engine Ticket governs wherever the Penalty or Rules sheets differ: meal allowance is $15.54, travel is $0.99 per km, and sleepover disturbance is paid at the overtime rate. The figures in this document have been reconciled to the Ticket.

Ordinary | 06:00 – 20:00 | 1 | 1.25
Evening | finishes between 20:00-24:00 | 1.125 | 1.375
Night | 00:00 – 06:00 | 1.15 | 1.4
5. Partitioning Logic for Developers
Boundary | Logic Name | Action
20:00:00 | The Evening Trigger | Look-Back Logic: If the shift continues past this point (and it's a weekday), all previous hours in that specific engagement (back to the start or last reset) are "upgraded" to the Evening Rate.
0:00:00 | The Midnight Pivot | Calendar Split: Mandatory split for accounting. Multipliers must be re-evaluated against the new calendar day (e.g., Friday Night 1.40x becomes Saturday 1.75x).
6:00:00 | The Morning Reset | Hard Reset: The Night Rate (1.40x/1.15x) expires. All subsequent hours must drop to the Ordinary Rate (1.25x/1.00x) unless it is a weekend.
6. Calculation Benchmark (Validation Case)
Scenario: 18 Dec, 9:30 PM – 19 Dec, 6:30 AM (Active Night) | Base: $35.67 (Casual)
Segment | Logic Applied | Multiplier | Total
21:30 - 00:00 | Evening (Ends > 20:00) | 1.375 | $122.62
00:00 - 06:00 | Night Loading | 1.4 | $299.63
06:00 - 06:30 | Normal Reset | 1.25 | $22.29
TOTAL  $444.54
7. Developer Mandatory Constraints
Highest Rate Wins: Rates never compound. If a segment is both "Evening" (1.375) and "Over 12h Span" (2.0), the engine must apply 2.0.
Broken Shift Separation: The 12-hour span includes unpaid breaks, but the penalty applies only to worked minutes.

fields.title: SCHADS Penalty Rates & Allowances
fields.content:
SCHADS Penalty Rates & Allowances

Authoritative source — SCHADS engine Ticket. The engine Ticket governs wherever the Penalty or Rules sheets differ: meal allowance is $15.54, travel is $0.99 per km, and sleepover disturbance is paid at the overtime rate. The figures in this document have been reconciled to the Ticket.

Penalty multipliers by shift type for permanent/full-time and casual employees, plus the full list of allowance amounts.

Shift type | Part-time/Full-time multiplier | Casual multiplier | Comments | Allowances
Weekday span and core penalties | Allowances | Rate
Ordinary hours (Mon–Fri, 6am–8pm) | 1 | 1.25 | Broken shift allowance - 1 unpaid break | $20.82 per broken shift
Afternoon shift (finishes after 8pm, before midnight) | 1.125 | 1.375 | Broken shift allowance - 2 unpaid breaks | $27.56 per broken shift
Night shift (finishes after midnight or starts before 6am) | 1.15 | 1.4 | First aid allowance - full-time | $20.46 per week
Weekends and public holidays | First aid allowance - part-time or casual | $0.54 per hour up to a maximum of $20.46 per week
Saturday | 1.5 | 1.75 | Any shift worked on a Saturday, starting at 12:AM Saturday night/Sunday morning | Hot work allowance - 40°C-46°C (employed at current workplace before 8 August 1991) | $0.61 per hour
Sunday | 2 | 2.25 | Any shift worked on a Saturday, starting at 12:AM Friday night/Saturday morning | Hot work allowance - over 46° (employed at current workplace before 8 August 1991) | $0.73 per hour
Public holiday | 2.5 | 2.75 | Any shift worked between 12 midnight on the night prior to the public holiday and 12 midnight of the public holiday | Laundry allowance | $0.32 per shift up to a maximum of $1.49 per week
Remote work | Laundry allowance - not a uniform | $0.32 per shift
Daytime | 1.25 | 1.5 | 100*1.75 | Meal allowance | 15.54
Evening Shift | 1.375 | 1.625 | On call allowance - any 24 hour period or part thereof when on call between the time of finishing ordinary duty on Monday to the time of finishing ordinary duty on Friday | $24.50
Night shift | 1.4 | 1.65 | On call allowance - any other 24 hour period or public holiday, or part thereof | 48.51
Saturday—within span (6am–8pm) | 1.75 | 2 | Sleepover allowance | $60.02 for each sleepover
Saturday—first 2 hrs outside span | 1.75 | 2 | Special or protective clothing reimbursement | reimbursement for any rubber gloves, special clothing or safety equipment the employee is required to provide
Saturday—after 2 hrs outside span | 1.75 | 2 | Uniform allowance | $1.23 per shift up to a maximum of $6.24 per week
Sunday | 2.25 | 2.5 | Repair and replacement of clothing reimbursement - not a uniform | reimbursement for the reasonable cost incurred in repairing or replacing the clothing with a substitute item
Public holiday | 2.75 | 3 | Telephone reimbursement - maintain telephone (on call) | reimbursement for rental charges
More than 10 hours a day—first 2 hrs | 1.75 | 2 | Telephone reimbursement - install and maintain telephone (on call) | reimbursement for the cost of installation and rental charges
More than 10 hours a day—after 2 hrs | 2.25 | 2.5 | Overnight away from home reimbursement | reimbursement for the cost of reasonable accommodation and meals
Weekday Overtime (Mon–Sat) | Travelling expenses reimbursement | reimbursement for the agreed cost of reasonable fares, meals and accommodation expenses
First block (first 3 hrs FT, first 2 hrs PT/disability services) | 1.5 | 1.75 | Vehicle allowance | $0.99 per km
After block (after 3 hrs FT, after 2 hrs PT/disability services) | 2 | 2.25
Public holiday overtime | 2.5 | 2.75
Less than 10-hour break after working overtime | 2 | 2.25
Allowances
Broken shifts

fields.title: SCHADS Calculation Conditions & Pay Formula
fields.content:
SCHADS Calculation Conditions & Pay Formula

Authoritative source — SCHADS engine Ticket. The engine Ticket governs wherever the Penalty or Rules sheets differ: meal allowance is $15.54, travel is $0.99 per km, and sleepover disturbance is paid at the overtime rate. The figures in this document have been reconciled to the Ticket.

The condition checklist, the unified pay formula and worked calculation examples.

Question / Logic  Action if "Yes"
Order | Check Category | Condition / Logic | Action if "Yes"
3 | Sleepover | 8-hr period on-site? | Add $60.02; Ensure pre/post work total $\ge$ 4 hrs.
1 | Holiday | Is the date a Public Holiday? | Use 2.5 (FT/PT) or 2.75 (Casual) for ALL hours.
2 | Safety | < 10 hrs break since last shift? | Use 2.0 (FT/PT) or 2.25 (Casual) for ALL hours.
4 | Broken Shift | Broken Shift | Add $20.82 (1 break) or $27.56 (2 breaks).
5 | 12hr Span | (Final Finish - First Start) > 12hrs? | Hours after 12th hr = P. Use 2.0 (FT/PT) or 2.25 (C).
6 | Min. Engagement | Segment < 2 hrs (Home Care)? | Round that segment duration up to 2.0.
7 | Daily OT | Actual work > 10 hrs in one day? | Use 1.5/2.0 (FT/PT) or 1.75/2.25 (Casual).
8 | Weekly OT | Cumulative hours > 38/week? | Use 1.5/2.0 (FT/PT) or 1.75/2.25 (Casual).
$$Total Pay = (Work\ hours \times B \times M) + \$60.02 + Allowances$$
Example: Work 5pm–10pm, Sleepover, Work 6am–9am.
Sleepover Check: Yes. Add $60.02.
Min Engagement: Worked 5 + 3 = 8 hours total (Exceeds the 4-hour minimum).
Broken Shift Check: No (A sleepover is not a "break" in a broken shift; it is its own category).
12hr Span: From 5pm start, the 12hr mark is 5am. Work from 6am–9am (3 hours) is after the 12hr span.
Calculation:
Before Sleepover: 5 hrs × $B$ × Evening/Night Rate.
After Sleepover: 3 hrs × $B$ × 2.25 (Span Penalty).
Allowance: + $60.02.
Segment | Label | Time | Multiplier | Calculation | Subtotal
9:30 PM – 12:00 AM | E ( Evening) | 2.50 hrs | 2.75 | $2.50 \times 35.67 \times 2.75$ | $245.23
12:00 AM – 6:00 AM | AN ( Active Night) | 6.00 hrs | 2.75 | $6.00 \times 35.67 \times 2.75$ | $588.56
6:00 AM – 6:30 AM | D ( Daytime) | 0.50 hrs | 2.75 | $0.50 \times 35.67 \times 2.75$ | $49.05
TOTALS | 9.00 hrs | $882.84
The Unified Payout Formula
To calculate your total pay ($P$), apply the specific hours to their corresponding rates:
$$P = \underbrace{(D \times B \times M_d) + (E \times B \times M_e) + (AN \times B \times M_n)}_{\text{Ordinary & Shift Loading}} + \underbrace{(P \times B \times M_p)}_{\text{Span Penalty}} + \underbrace{Allowances}_{\text{Broken/Sleep/Travel}}$$
Formula Application Rules:
Highest Rate Wins: If an hour is both AN (Night) and P (Span Penalty), use the higher multiplier (e.g., for a Casual, 2.25 is higher than 1.40).
Public Holiday Override: If the day is a Public Holiday, ignore D, E, AN, and P. Use PH for every hour.
The "P" Variable: The moment you cross the 12-hour mark from your first start of the day, all subsequent work hours are switched to the P rate.
With Sleepover:
Segment 1 (E): 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Work)
Sleepover Period: 10:00 PM – 6:00 AM (Sleeping)
Segment 2 (D/P): 6:00 AM – 9:00 AM (Work)
Step 1: Run the Condition Checkers
Sleepover? Yes. Add $60.02 allowance.
Minimum Engagement? Worked 5h + 3h = 8h. (Passed the 4h minimum).
12-Hour Span? Start is 5:00 PM. The 12-hour limit is 5:00 AM.
Penalty Trigger: Because the 12-hour mark passed while you were sleeping, all work upon waking up (6:00 AM) is now P (Span Penalty).
Step 2: Categorize the Variables
E (Evening): 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM (5 hours) → Multiplier 1.375
AN (Active Night): 0 hours (You were sleeping).
P (Span Penalty): 6:00 AM – 9:00 AM (3 hours) → Multiplier 2.25
Allowance: Sleepover Allowance → $60.02
Step 3: Apply the Formula
$$Total Pay = (E \times B \times 1.375) + (P \times B \times 2.25) + \$60.02$$
The Calculation:
Segment | Label | Calculation | Subtotal

fields.title: SCHADS Calculation Conditions & Pay Formula
fields.content:
SCHADS Calculation Conditions & Pay Formula (continued)

Authoritative source — SCHADS engine Ticket. The engine Ticket governs wherever the Penalty or Rules sheets differ: meal allowance is $15.54, travel is $0.99 per km, and sleepover disturbance is paid at the overtime rate. The figures in this document have been reconciled to the Ticket.

Step 3: Apply the Formula
$$Total Pay = (E \times B \times 1.375) + (P \times B \times 2.25) + \$60.02$$
The Calculation:
Segment | Label | Calculation | Subtotal
Evening Work | E | 5-10 | 5.00 \times 35.67 \times 1.375$ | $245.23
Sleepover | Fixed | 10-6 | Allowance Amount10-6 | $60.02
Morning Work | P | 6-9 | 3.00 \times 35.67 \times 2.25$ | $240.77
TOTAL PAY  $546.02
Comparison: What if you were Full-Time?
If you were Full-Time ($B = 35.00$), the multipliers change but the logic remains the same:
E: $5 \times 35 \times 1.125 = \$196.88$
Sleepover: $\$60.02$
P (Span Penalty): $3 \times 35 \times 2.0 = \$210.00$
Total: $466.90
Important: The "Sleep Disturbance" Add-on
If a client woke you up for 30 minutes at 2:00 AM:
Condition: Disturbance must be paid at a minimum of 1 hour.
Multiplier: Since it's between 10 PM and 6 AM, it's Night/OT. Most systems apply the 2.25 (Casual) or 2.0 (FT) overtime rate for disturbances.
Impact: You would add $(1 \times 35.67 \times 2.25) = \mathbf{+\$80.26}$ to the total above.
Segment | Label | Time | Multiplier | Calculation | Subtotal
Evening Work | E | 5.0 hrs | 1.375 | $5 \times 35.67 \times 1.375$ | $245.23
Sleepover | Fixed | N/A | N/A | Allowance | $60.02
Disturbance | SD | 1.0 hr* | 2.25 | $1 \times 35.67 \times 2.25$ | $80.26
Morning Work | P | 3.0 hrs | 2.25 | $2 \times 35.67 \times 2.25$ | $160.52
TOTAL PAY  $546.03
